_note: Acceptance criteria for the live performance-acceptance harness (Context B; cabal executable perf-acceptance, make perf-acceptance). Version-controlled on purpose: moving the bar is an explicit, reviewed act. `defaultBudgetMs` is the maximum Ecluse work-per-request overhead (decode -> project -> rule sweep -> filter/rewrite -> re-serialise) over a single live packument before the run reds; `perPackageBudgetMs` raises it for the heavy, many-version packuments whose processing is legitimately costlier. A breach reds the (non-required) check to prompt a human decision — code regression vs reality outgrew the budget — and never blocks merge. These bounds are calibrated from a first real run with deliberately generous headroom (roughly 3x or more over the measured overhead) to absorb shared-runner noise and real-world packument growth; tighten or relax them as the real runs accrue — that is what version-controlling them is for. See docs/architecture/performance.md.
